mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate and eases financial conditions, which raises S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is falsified if rate cuts do not coincide with positive forward equity performance.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS declines on a monthly basis and the next 20-trading-day SPX return is negative in most such instances, or rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL reading that rises faster than the prior 3-month trend indicates inflation pressure that increases rate-hike risk and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rises above its 12-month median and FEDFUNDS is flat or rising
regime_hint: inflation scare with policy tightening risk
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-surprise episodes followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign remains nonnegative after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and demand, which lowers expected earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in a majority of instances where UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp over 3 months, or if the rolling 3-year IC is above zero.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth slows while UNRATE rises, the labor market is losing momentum and earnings expectations weaken, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days should be negative. The signal requires both payroll deterioration and unemployment deterioration together.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth turns negative and UNRATE rises over the same window
failure_condition: Reject if the combined payroll-plus-unemployment trigger does not outperform each series alone, or if triggered periods fail to show negative 20-day SPX returns relative to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO reflects stronger industrial output and supply-side activity, which supports cyclical earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher INDPRO growth is followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the sign flips across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in HOUST above trend indicates stronger residential demand and construction activity, which supports broader cyclical growth and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 5% versus its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakouts are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in most cases, or if the breakout effect disappears after a 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized acceleration in GDPC1 and RSAFS identifies broad final demand growth, which should raise expected corporate revenues and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect is strongest only when both real GDP and retail sales improve together.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth is positive and RSAFS 3-month growth accelerates above its prior 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDP-and-retail acceleration fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns, or if either proxy alone explains the effect equally well without interaction.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate regime, an elevated FEDFUNDS level combined with sticky CPIAUCSL keeps real policy tight and depresses equity multiples, so S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days should be negative. The signal weakens once policy stops tightening or inflation decelerates materially.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation remains above its 6-month trend
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not negative during high-rate, sticky-inflation episodes, or if the signal vanishes when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% but CPI inflation is subdued.
